8.1.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.1.title: Board Approval of Technology Risk Appetite

business.summary: Without active board oversight of technology risk, the institution faces uncontrolled exposure to cyber threats, system failures, and regulatory penalties that can erode customer trust and shareholder value.

requirements.requirement: Allocate a dedicated annual budget for technology risk management activities, including cybersecurity tools, skilled personnel, and third-party assessments, with board-level approval and quarterly spend tracking
requirements.rationale: Underfunding technology risk leaves the institution vulnerable to breaches and outages that cost far more than prevention; BNM expects visible resource commitment as evidence of board seriousness

requirements.requirement: Integrate technology risk as a standing agenda item in board meetings with dedicated discussion time, not relegated to Any Other Business
requirements.rationale: Technology risk that is buried under general items receives insufficient board attention, leading to uninformed decision-making on matters that can disrupt the entire institution

requirements.requirement: Ensure technology risk is formally embedded within the enterprise risk management framework with clear linkages to credit, market, and operational risk categories
requirements.rationale: Treating technology risk in isolation creates blind spots; interconnected risks such as cyber-enabled fraud or system outages affecting trading platforms require an integrated view for effective board oversight

technology.summary: IT teams must provide the board with accurate, timely data on the institution's technology risk posture through dashboards, metrics, and reporting systems that translate technical risks into business impact language.

requirements.requirement: Implement a technology risk dashboard or reporting platform that aggregates key risk indicators across infrastructure, applications, and cybersecurity domains for board-level consumption
requirements.rationale: The board cannot exercise effective oversight without consolidated, accurate, and current data on the institution's technology risk landscape presented in business-relevant terms

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive technology asset inventory that underpins risk assessments and enables the board to understand the scope and criticality of technology dependencies
requirements.rationale: You cannot manage risks to assets you do not know exist; an accurate inventory is the foundation for all technology risk reporting to the board

requirements.requirement: Establish automated monitoring and alerting for critical technology risk indicators such as system availability, security incidents, and patch compliance rates
requirements.rationale: Manual monitoring is insufficient for the speed and volume of modern technology threats; automated systems ensure the board receives timely and accurate risk data

governance.summary: Formal governance structures must ensure the board has clear authority, defined responsibilities, and documented processes for overseeing technology risk across the institution.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a Board Technology Risk Charter that defines the board's roles, responsibilities, and authority in overseeing technology risk management
requirements.rationale: Without a formal charter, accountability for technology risk oversight becomes ambiguous, and BNM examiners will question whether the board takes its oversight obligations seriously

requirements.requirement: Document and enforce a formal escalation framework that ensures material technology risk events are reported to the board within defined timeframes
requirements.rationale: Delayed escalation of critical technology incidents can result in regulatory breaches and reputational damage; the board must be informed promptly to fulfil its oversight role

requirements.requirement: Require board sign-off on the technology risk management framework, risk appetite statement, and any material changes to the institution's technology risk profile
requirements.rationale: Board approval creates accountability and ensures alignment between strategic direction and technology risk tolerance, which is a core BNM expectation under RMiT

8.2.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.2.title: Board Oversight Responsibilities

business.summary: A dedicated board-level technology risk committee ensures focused oversight of technology threats that could disrupt banking operations, compromise customer data, or trigger regulatory sanctions.

requirements.requirement: Establish a board-level technology risk committee with a formal charter that defines its scope, authority, and relationship to other board committees such as the Board Risk Committee and Audit Committee
requirements.rationale: Without a dedicated committee, technology risk competes for attention with credit, market, and other risks, leading to insufficient scrutiny of an area that increasingly drives operational and reputational exposure

requirements.requirement: Ensure the committee meets at least quarterly with documented agendas covering cybersecurity posture, technology incidents, risk appetite adherence, and emerging technology threats
requirements.rationale: Quarterly meetings are the BNM-mandated minimum; less frequent meetings leave gaps in board awareness during periods when the threat landscape evolves rapidly

requirements.requirement: Allocate budget for external technology and cybersecurity advisors to supplement committee expertise where internal knowledge gaps exist
requirements.rationale: Malaysian banking institutions may not always have board members with deep technical expertise; external advisors ensure the committee can effectively challenge management on complex technology risk matters

technology.summary: Technology teams must provide the committee with structured reporting, data-driven insights, and access to subject matter experts to enable informed oversight decisions.

requirements.requirement: Develop standardised committee reporting packs that include technology risk dashboards, incident summaries, vulnerability metrics, and risk treatment progress updates
requirements.rationale: Consistent, structured reporting enables the committee to track trends over time and identify emerging issues before they escalate into material incidents

requirements.requirement: Provide the committee with access to technology risk management tools and platforms for independent verification of reported metrics when requested
requirements.rationale: Committee members may need to validate management-reported data; access to source systems supports the independent oversight role expected by BNM

governance.summary: Formal governance arrangements must ensure the committee operates with proper authority, documented processes, and qualified membership to fulfil its RMiT oversight mandate.

requirements.requirement: Document and maintain committee terms of reference specifying membership composition, quorum requirements, meeting frequency, reporting lines to the full board, and authority to commission independent reviews
requirements.rationale: Clear terms of reference are the primary document BNM examiners review to assess whether the committee has the mandate and authority to fulfil its oversight role effectively

requirements.requirement: Maintain documented profiles of all committee members demonstrating relevant technology, cybersecurity, or risk management expertise through qualifications, certifications, or professional experience
requirements.rationale: BNM explicitly requires committee members to have sufficient knowledge in technology and cybersecurity; inability to demonstrate this competency is a common examination finding

requirements.requirement: Maintain complete records of meeting attendance, ensuring quorum is consistently achieved and that attendance rates are reported to the full board annually
requirements.rationale: Poor attendance undermines the committee's effectiveness and signals to BNM that technology risk oversight is not taken seriously at the board level

8.3.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.3.title: Board-Level Technology Committee

business.summary: Board members who lack technology and cybersecurity knowledge cannot effectively challenge management or make informed decisions about technology investments, risk appetite, and incident response, exposing the institution to poorly governed technology risk.

requirements.requirement: Conduct an annual board skills assessment to identify technology and cybersecurity knowledge gaps and develop a targeted training plan to address them
requirements.rationale: Without understanding their own competency gaps, the board cannot take corrective action; BNM expects the board to proactively invest in its collective technology literacy

requirements.requirement: Engage qualified external technology and cybersecurity advisors to provide independent counsel to the board on complex technology risk matters
requirements.rationale: Not every board member will become a technology expert; external advisors bridge competency gaps and provide the independent perspective needed for effective board challenge
requirements.frequency: Annual engagement review, Ad hoc as needed

requirements.requirement: Include technology and cybersecurity competency as a criterion in the board nomination and selection process for new directors
requirements.rationale: Proactively recruiting directors with technology expertise is more effective than retroactively training directors without any technology background, and demonstrates forward-looking governance to BNM

technology.summary: Technology leaders must support board education by delivering accessible briefings, organising hands-on demonstrations, and translating complex technical concepts into business-relevant language.

requirements.requirement: Develop and deliver at least two board education sessions annually on emerging technology risks, threat landscape updates, and cybersecurity trends relevant to Malaysian financial services
requirements.rationale: Regular education sessions keep the board informed of evolving threats such as ransomware, supply chain attacks, and AI-driven fraud that require board-level awareness and strategic response

requirements.requirement: Provide the board with access to curated threat intelligence briefings and industry reports tailored to the Malaysian banking sector
requirements.rationale: Board members need context-specific information about threats targeting Malaysian financial institutions to make informed risk appetite and investment decisions

governance.summary: Formal governance mechanisms must ensure board technology competency is systematically assessed, developed, and maintained through structured training programmes and documented competency frameworks.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board Technology Competency Framework that defines expected knowledge areas, proficiency levels, and assessment criteria for all board members
requirements.rationale: A structured framework transforms competency development from ad hoc to systematic, providing BNM with clear evidence that the institution takes board technology literacy seriously

requirements.requirement: Maintain detailed training records for all board members documenting attendance at technology and cybersecurity education sessions, external conferences, and certification programmes
requirements.rationale: Training records are primary evidence for BNM examiners assessing compliance with this guideline; without them, the institution cannot demonstrate ongoing board development

requirements.requirement: Report the results of board competency assessments and training completion rates to the Board Nomination Committee annually for inclusion in the annual governance review
requirements.rationale: Embedding competency results into the governance review process creates accountability and ensures technology knowledge gaps are addressed through the established board development pipeline

8.4.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.4.title: Board Discussion on Technology and Cyber Risk

business.summary: A clearly defined technology risk appetite statement ensures the institution takes calculated technology risks that align with its strategic objectives and regulatory obligations, preventing both excessive risk-taking and overly conservative approaches that hinder digital transformation.

requirements.requirement: Develop and obtain board approval for a technology risk appetite statement that defines acceptable levels of risk across cybersecurity, system availability, data integrity, and third-party technology dependencies
requirements.rationale: Without a board-approved risk appetite, management has no authoritative guidance on how much technology risk is acceptable, leading to inconsistent risk decisions across the institution

requirements.requirement: Express risk appetite using both quantitative metrics (e.g., maximum acceptable downtime, incident thresholds, recovery time objectives) and qualitative statements (e.g., zero tolerance for data breaches affecting customer records)
requirements.rationale: BNM explicitly requires both quantitative and qualitative measures; qualitative-only statements are too vague to operationalise, while numbers alone miss important nuances

requirements.requirement: Cascade the board-approved technology risk appetite into operational risk tolerances and limits for business units, IT, and cybersecurity teams
requirements.rationale: A risk appetite statement sitting in a boardroom document achieves nothing unless it is translated into operational limits that guide day-to-day decision-making across the institution

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement monitoring systems that continuously measure actual risk levels against approved appetite thresholds and trigger alerts when limits are approached or breached.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated monitoring dashboards that track actual technology risk levels against approved appetite thresholds in real time or near-real time
requirements.rationale: Manual tracking of risk appetite adherence is too slow to detect breaches in a rapidly evolving threat environment; automated monitoring enables timely escalation and response

requirements.requirement: Configure alert mechanisms that notify risk management and senior management when technology risk indicators approach or breach defined appetite thresholds
requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting when approaching thresholds allows corrective action before a formal breach occurs, demonstrating mature risk management to BNM

requirements.requirement: Maintain historical data on risk appetite adherence to support trend analysis and inform annual risk appetite recalibration
requirements.rationale: Historical trend data enables the board to make evidence-based decisions when reviewing and adjusting risk appetite, rather than relying on subjective assessments

governance.summary: Governance processes must ensure the risk appetite statement is formally approved, regularly reviewed, effectively communicated, and used as the basis for risk decision-making throughout the institution.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a formal annual review of the technology risk appetite statement to ensure it reflects changes in the operating environment, threat landscape, regulatory requirements, and business strategy
requirements.rationale: A static risk appetite statement becomes irrelevant as the institution's technology landscape evolves; annual review ensures alignment with current realities and BNM expectations

requirements.requirement: Document and distribute the approved risk appetite statement to all relevant stakeholders including business unit heads, IT leadership, and risk management teams with formal acknowledgement
requirements.rationale: Risk appetite is only effective if the people making technology risk decisions are aware of and understand the board-approved boundaries within which they must operate

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal process for risk appetite threshold breaches that includes root cause analysis, remediation planning, and board notification within defined timeframes
requirements.rationale: Threshold breaches without a structured response process indicate that the risk appetite framework is not operationally embedded, which is a significant BNM examination finding

8.5.subsection: Responsibilities of the Board of Directors
8.5.title: Board Audit Committee Technology Oversight

business.summary: Without comprehensive and timely technology risk reporting, the board operates blind to cyber threats, system vulnerabilities, and remediation gaps that could result in service disruptions, customer harm, or BNM enforcement actions.

requirements.requirement: Ensure senior management delivers comprehensive quarterly technology risk reports to the board covering key risk indicators, incident summaries, risk mitigation progress, and forward-looking threat assessments
requirements.rationale: Quarterly reporting is the BNM-mandated minimum cadence; less frequent reporting creates unacceptable gaps in board awareness of the institution's technology risk posture

requirements.requirement: Include trend analysis in all board technology risk reports showing directional movement of key risk indicators over at least four quarters
requirements.rationale: Point-in-time snapshots do not reveal whether the institution's technology risk posture is improving or deteriorating; trend analysis enables the board to assess the effectiveness of risk management investments

requirements.requirement: Include forward-looking assessments in board reports covering emerging technology threats, planned technology changes, and their potential impact on the institution's risk profile
requirements.rationale: BNM expects boards to be proactive, not reactive; forward-looking assessments demonstrate that the institution anticipates and prepares for evolving technology risks rather than only responding to incidents

technology.summary: Technology and security teams must build robust data collection, analytics, and reporting capabilities to produce accurate, timely, and board-appropriate technology risk reports.

requirements.requirement: Implement a centralised key risk indicator dashboard that aggregates data from security, infrastructure, and application monitoring systems into a unified board reporting view
requirements.rationale: Fragmented data across multiple systems leads to inconsistent and potentially inaccurate board reporting; a centralised dashboard ensures a single source of truth for technology risk metrics

requirements.requirement: Automate the collection and correlation of technology risk data to minimise manual data gathering and reduce the risk of reporting errors or delays
requirements.rationale: Manual data collection for board reports is error-prone and time-consuming; automation ensures accuracy and allows the risk team to focus on analysis rather than data aggregation

requirements.requirement: Develop incident summary reporting capabilities that categorise technology incidents by severity, root cause, business impact, and resolution status for board consumption
requirements.rationale: The board needs structured incident data to understand patterns, assess the effectiveness of incident response, and make informed decisions about investing in preventive controls

governance.summary: Formal governance processes must ensure board reports are standardised, timely, reviewed for accuracy, and result in documented board acknowledgement and action.

requirements.requirement: Establish a standardised board reporting template for technology risk that covers all mandatory elements: KRIs, incident summaries, risk mitigation status, trend analysis, and forward-looking assessments
requirements.rationale: A standardised template ensures consistency across reporting periods and prevents omission of critical information elements required by BNM

requirements.requirement: Implement a quality assurance process for board technology risk reports including review by the CISO and CRO before submission to ensure accuracy and completeness
requirements.rationale: Inaccurate or incomplete board reports undermine trust and can lead to poor board decisions; a formal QA process ensures the board receives reliable information

requirements.requirement: Document board acknowledgement and any resulting actions or directives arising from technology risk reports in board meeting minutes
requirements.rationale: BNM examiners look for evidence that the board not only receives reports but engages with the content and directs action; undocumented acknowledgement is equivalent to no acknowledgement

8.6.title: Senior Management Accountability for Technology Risk

business.summary: Clear senior management accountability for technology risk ensures that the board-approved framework is operationally implemented, with a designated CISO providing independent cybersecurity leadership and direct board access.

requirements.requirement: Define and document a senior management accountability matrix that assigns specific technology risk responsibilities to named C-suite executives with clear ownership boundaries
requirements.rationale: Ambiguous accountability leads to gaps in technology risk management where critical activities fall between roles; BNM expects named individuals accountable for specific risk domains

requirements.requirement: Formally appoint a Chief Information Security Officer or equivalent role with a mandate covering cybersecurity strategy, incident response oversight, and security architecture governance
requirements.rationale: The CISO role is explicitly required by BNM RMiT; absence of a formally appointed CISO with a clear mandate is a direct compliance gap and a common examination finding

requirements.requirement: Ensure the CISO has a direct reporting line to the board or board technology risk committee for cybersecurity matters, independent of the CTO or Head of IT reporting structure
requirements.rationale: If the CISO reports only through the CTO, there is an inherent conflict of interest as the technology delivery function controls the security oversight function; BNM requires independent access to the board

technology.summary: Technology leadership must implement the operational structures, reporting mechanisms, and performance management systems that translate senior management accountability into effective day-to-day technology risk management.

requirements.requirement: Implement a technology risk management reporting system that provides the CISO with real-time visibility across all technology risk domains to support board and committee reporting obligations
requirements.rationale: The CISO cannot fulfil board reporting responsibilities without comprehensive visibility into the institution's technology risk posture across all domains including infrastructure, applications, and third parties

requirements.requirement: Ensure the CISO has administrative and audit access to all security tools, SIEM platforms, and vulnerability management systems to independently verify the institution's security posture
requirements.rationale: A CISO who cannot independently verify security data reported by operational teams cannot provide credible assurance to the board; tool access supports the independence requirement

governance.summary: Governance structures must formalise senior management accountability through documented roles, reporting lines, and performance frameworks that demonstrate compliance with BNM's expectations for technology risk leadership.

requirements.requirement: Document the CISO's appointment, reporting lines, mandate, and authority in a formal board-approved mandate document that is reviewed annually
requirements.rationale: A formal mandate document is the primary evidence BNM examiners seek to verify that the CISO role has been established with appropriate authority and independence as required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Include technology risk management performance objectives in senior management KPIs and performance assessments to reinforce accountability
requirements.rationale: Accountability without performance consequences is hollow; embedding technology risk objectives in KPIs ensures senior leaders are personally invested in effective risk management

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of CISO presentations and reports to the board or board committee, demonstrating that the direct reporting line is exercised in practice, not just on paper
requirements.rationale: BNM examiners will verify that the CISO's board reporting line is actively used; a reporting line that exists in the organisation chart but is never exercised fails to meet the intent of the requirement

business.summary: An independent technology risk management function acts as the institution's second line of defence, providing objective risk identification, assessment, and reporting that is free from the conflicts inherent in technology operations teams managing their own risks.

requirements.requirement: Establish and fund a dedicated technology risk management function that is organisationally independent from IT operations, with its own budget, staffing, and reporting line
requirements.rationale: If technology risk management reports to the same leader who manages IT operations, there is an inherent conflict of interest; independence ensures objective risk assessment and reporting

requirements.requirement: Staff the technology risk management function with personnel who possess relevant risk management, cybersecurity, and technology audit competencies proportionate to the institution's technology footprint
requirements.rationale: An understaffed or underqualified function cannot effectively identify and assess technology risks across the institution's growing technology estate, creating blind spots that BNM will identify during examinations

requirements.requirement: Ensure the function has unrestricted access to all technology systems, data, documentation, and personnel necessary to perform its risk identification and assessment activities
requirements.rationale: A risk function that cannot access the systems and information it needs to assess risk is functionally ineffective; BNM expects demonstrated access authority, not just a paper mandate

technology.summary: The technology risk management function requires dedicated tools, platforms, and system access to independently identify, assess, monitor, and report technology risks across the institution.

requirements.requirement: Provide the technology risk management function with a dedicated GRC platform or risk management toolset for documenting risk assessments, tracking treatment plans, and generating risk reports
requirements.rationale: Spreadsheet-based risk management is error-prone and does not scale; a dedicated platform enables consistent methodology application, audit trails, and efficient reporting to the board

requirements.requirement: Grant the function read-only access to technology monitoring systems including SIEM, vulnerability scanners, and configuration management databases for independent risk verification
requirements.rationale: Independent access to operational monitoring data enables the risk function to verify claims made by IT operations and identify risks that operational teams may not self-report

governance.summary: Formal governance arrangements must establish the function's mandate, authority, independence, and reporting obligations through documented charters and organisational structures approved by the board.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal charter for the technology risk management function that defines its mandate, authority, independence, scope, and reporting obligations, approved by the board or board committee
requirements.rationale: The charter is the foundational document that BNM examiners review to assess whether the function has the authority and independence required by RMiT; without it, the function's legitimacy is questionable

requirements.requirement: Ensure the function's organisational placement demonstrates clear independence from IT operations through separate reporting lines, as evidenced in the organisation chart
requirements.rationale: Independence is not just about the charter; BNM examiners will verify the actual organisation structure to confirm the function does not report to IT operations leadership

requirements.requirement: Maintain a staffing plan and competency matrix for the function that maps required competencies against current staff capabilities and identifies development needs
requirements.rationale: BNM expects the function to have adequate expertise; a competency matrix demonstrates systematic capability management and highlights any gaps that need to be addressed through hiring or training

requirements.requirement: Define and implement regular reporting from the technology risk function to the CRO, senior management, and the board committee covering risk assessments, emerging risks, and function performance metrics
requirements.rationale: Regular reporting demonstrates that the function is active and producing value; BNM will question a function that exists on paper but produces no visible output or reporting
